stem: What is an x-coordinate of an x-intercept of the graph of y = 4(x - 7)(x + 9)(x - 2) in the xy-plane?
rationale: The x-intercepts of the graph occur where y = 0. Setting 4(x - 7)(x + 9)(x - 2) = 0 and applying the zero-product property gives x - 7 = 0, x + 9 = 0, or x - 2 = 0. So the x-coordinates of the x-intercepts are 7, -9, and 2. Any one of these values is correct.
